props_visible: Traditional Najdi clay/mud-brick facade exterior with triangular cut-out patterns (backlit orange)
notable_visual_elements: Triangular cut-out pattern on exterior wall = signature Najdi/Diriyah heritage architecture motif
Clay-orange facade = traditional Najdi building material color
Interior dark/warm framing the bright exterior creates cinematic depth
Kilim chair upholstery = heritage textile integration in modern furniture
This is a new branch or the same branch — shows outdoor/covered terrace with authentic architecture backdrop

hospitality_cues: Architecture as hospitality environment — the building itself signals 'you are in a Najdi space'
cultural_notes.free_notes: The triangular cut-out patterns (شرافات/شرف) are the single most recognizable Najdi architectural motif — found on traditional Diriyah buildings, old Riyadh houses, and heritage palaces. @aseeb.najd's location within or adjacent to a building using this motif is a profound location choice. The clay-colored facade and mashrabiya lattice are architectural statements of Najdi identity that no amount of interior decoration can substitute for.

pattern_matches.notes: Classic interior-exterior depth shot — traditional Najdi architecture as the backdrop hero
Setting communicates 'you dine inside authentic Najdi heritage' — environment is the brand
